title: rubus fruticosus subsp. radula
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. radula, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan dewberry, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. radula include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes and arthritis. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
